title: Local and CI test runner compatibility
goal: Make the Leitstand test/run instructions account for the observed local Node 22/V8 crash and Vitest WebAssembly limitation without weakening CI evidence.

acceptance.assertion: The task records whether the Node 22 crash is local-host specific, package-version related, or expected under the current environment.
Recommended local test commands are executable on the Heim-PC or document a known fallback that does not pretend full Vitest success.
GitHub CI remains the source of truth for full test pass/fail; no test is skipped merely to hide the local runner issue.

metadata.priority_reason: P2/P3: local validation is currently partially blocked; CI was green on main

local_diagnostics.plain_node_failure: V8 fatal error before repo code; SetPermissionsOnExecutableMemoryChunk
local_diagnostics.jitless_vitest: fails before tests with ReferenceError: WebAssembly is not defined

local_checks: acceptance checks for local diagnosis, command grounding, and ci-not-weakened

ci.full_vitest_truth: GitHub CI runs pnpm test on Node 20; PR did not modify CI to skip or weaken tests.

verification.verification_summary: Leitstand PR #159 added docs/runbooks/local-test-runner.md, updated README local validation guidance, and linked the runbook from docs/index.md. It documents the Heim-PC Node 22/V8 crash, the safe jitless lint/typecheck/build fallback, the Vitest WebAssembly limitation, and preserves GitHub CI as full test truth.

acceptance_evidence.local-runner-diagnosis: Node v22.23.1 default JIT path exits 133 before repo code; jitless node succeeds; jitless Vitest fails due missing WebAssembly.
